sources: Carnegie Mellon University / Educational Testing Service
Collaborative for Academic, Social, and Emotional Learning (CASEL)
National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine
U.S. Department of Education Office for Civil Rights

coverage.source: Collaborative for Academic, Social, and Emotional Learning (CASEL)
National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine

entries.title: Organizing Instruction and Study to Improve Student Learning
entries.desc: Seven recommendations for using learning and study time effectively in subjects requiring content mastery (science, social studies, mathematics): spacing content review, alternating worked examples with practice problems, combining visual and verbal instruction, linking abstract and concrete concepts, strategic use of quizzes, efficient study time allocation, and deep explanatory questioning.

entries.title: Using Student Achievement Data to Support Instructional Decision Making
entries.desc: Five recommendations for leveraging student data to monitor academic progress and evaluate teaching: establishing schoolwide data systems, fostering a data-informed culture, integrating data review into instructional improvement cycles, teaching students to interpret their own performance data for goal-setting, and implementing district-level evidence infrastructure.

entries.title: Improving Mathematical Problem Solving in Grades 4 Through 8
entries.desc: Prepared by Mathematica. Five recommendations for teachers, math coaches, and curriculum developers targeting grades 4–8: preparing problems for whole-class instruction, supporting student self-monitoring and reflection, teaching visual representation, exposing students to multiple solution strategies, and helping students recognize mathematical concepts and notation.

entries.title: Teaching Math to Young Children
entries.desc: Five recommendations for preschool, prekindergarten, and kindergarten educators: teaching number and operations using developmental progression (Moderate Evidence), teaching geometry and patterns developmentally (Minimal Evidence), using progress monitoring aligned to individual child knowledge (Minimal Evidence), teaching mathematical perspectives on everyday situations (Minimal Evidence), and dedicating daily math time integrated throughout instruction (Minimal Evidence). Each recommendation includes implementation steps and solutions for common obstacles.

entries.title: Teaching Academic Content and Literacy to English Learners in Elementary and Middle School
entries.desc: Four recommendations for elementary and middle school educators: intensive academic vocabulary instruction using varied activities, integration of oral and written English instruction into content areas, structured opportunities for written language development, and small-group interventions for struggling learners. Includes examples aligned to Common Core standards.

entries.title: Teaching Strategies for Improving Algebra Knowledge in Middle and High School Students
entries.desc: Three recommendations for middle and high school algebra educators: analyzing algebraic reasoning, utilizing structural representations, and selecting alternative problem-solving approaches. Each recommendation includes implementation steps and common roadblocks.

entries.title: Using Technology to Support Postsecondary Student Learning
entries.desc: Five recommendations for higher education instructors, instructional designers, and administrators: use of communication tools, digital resources, self-regulated learning strategies, performance feedback, and simulation technologies for problem-solving. Targets postsecondary settings.

entries.title: Assisting Students Struggling with Mathematics: Intervention in the Elementary Grades
entries.desc: Six recommendations for grades K–6, all with strong evidence ratings: systematic instruction, mathematical language instruction, use of concrete representations, number line application, word problem instruction, and timed activities to build fluency. Covers both general student populations and children with disabilities.

entries.title: Preparing Young Children for School
entries.desc: Seven recommendations distilling contemporary early childhood and preschool research to help prepare young children to benefit from educational opportunities in school settings.

entries.title: CAST Universal Design for Learning Guidelines 3.0
entries.desc: Organization: CAST. An educational framework "based on scientific insights into how humans learn" designed as "a tool that can be applied in any discipline or domain to ensure that all learners are able to access and participate in meaningful, challenging learning opportunities." Version 3.0 specifically addresses "critical barriers rooted in biases and systems of exclusion for learners with and without disabilities." Three design principles: Engagement, Representation, and Action & Expression. Overarching goal: learner agency that is "purposeful & reflective, resourceful & authentic, strategic & action-oriented." Downloadable PDF available in 20+ languages. Audience: educators, curriculum developers, learning and instructional designers, researchers, parents.

entries.title: Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
entries.desc: Adopted 2021, published 2022. UNESCO's inaugural global standard on AI ethics, addressed to all 194 UNESCO member states. Establishes that "the protection of human rights and dignity is the cornerstone" of the framework. Covers transparency, fairness, and human oversight of AI systems across Policy Action Areas addressing data governance, environmental concerns, gender equity, education, research, health, and social wellbeing.

entries.title: UNESCO AI Competency Framework for Students
entries.desc: Four core competency areas for students (K-12 and higher education): "a human-centred mindset," "ethics of AI," "AI techniques and applications," and "AI system design." Direct PDF available in English, Arabic, French, Portuguese, Spanish, and Vietnamese at unesdoc.unesco.org (access from direct UNESDOC URL was blocked during audit; description sourced from UNESCO article page).

entries.title: UNESCO AI Competency Framework for Teachers
entries.desc: Five competency areas for teachers and educators: "a human-centred mindset," "ethics of AI," "AI foundations and applications," "AI pedagogy," and "AI for professional development." Direct PDF available in English, Arabic, French, Portuguese, Spanish, and Vietnamese at unesdoc.unesco.org (access from direct UNESDOC URL was blocked during audit; description sourced from UNESCO article page).

entries.title: EMERALDS: Preparation for Success in Algebra
entries.source: Carnegie Mellon University / Educational Testing Service
entries.desc: Principal Investigator: David C. Geary (University of Missouri). Institutions: Human-Computer Interaction Institute at Carnegie Mellon University and Educational Testing Service. "One of the largest longitudinal assessments of what mathematics prepares students for success in Algebra" — uses anonymized large-scale data to identify mathematical competencies in earlier grades that form critical foundations for algebraic success, with particular focus on historically underserved student populations. Report hosted at Achieve the Core; full report linked from that page.

entries.title: Lenses on Literature — Evidence for ESSA
entries.desc: Evidence review published at Evidence for ESSA (Johns Hopkins University). Carnegie Learning's Lenses on Literature (formerly Literacy Design Collaborative) earned a "Promising" (Tier 3) ESSA evidence rating. Instructional framework emphasizing daily, standards-driven reading, writing, and discourse organized around disciplinary literacy with complex texts. Primary study: 18,203 students across Kentucky and Pennsylvania (2012–2013). Effect size: +0.05 average; Kentucky: +0.06 (statistically significant); Pennsylvania: +0.03 (non-significant). Includes scaffolds for multilingual learners, embedded assessments using Stanford-aligned rubrics, and professional development with coaching and data analysis.

entries.title: The State of Educational Data Mining in 2009: A Review and Future Visions
entries.desc: Journal of Educational Data Mining, Vol. 1, No. 1. Inaugural-issue review paper for the EDM field: examines the history and methodological evolution of educational data mining, comparing early approaches with 2008–2009 research. Identifies three field-wide trends: increased emphasis on prediction methods, emergence of discovery-with-models techniques, and declining focus on relationship mining. Catalogues the most-cited EDM papers published 1995–2005 and analyzes their influence within and beyond the field. Open access under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0.

entries.title: Breaking With the Past: Embracing Digital Transformation in Education
entries.desc: Digital Promise report arguing for digital transformation of teaching and learning at scale across U.S. schools. Compares traditional one-size-fits-all instruction to learner-centered, personalized frameworks and examines how digital technologies can enable the transition—drawing on how other industries have used innovation to drive systemic change.

entries.title: Computational Thinking for an Inclusive World
entries.desc: Digital Promise report calling on educators to integrate computational thinking across PreK–12 disciplinary learning with an explicit equity focus, addressing persistent gaps in access to computing education for marginalized students. Argues that CT skills are important for all students—not only future computer science students—and issues two calls to action for designing inclusive computing learning opportunities.

entries.title: Shifting Mindsets: Designing Lessons for Learner Variability
entries.desc: Digital Promise pilot study conducted with DonorsChoose examining whether teachers could use the Learner Variability Navigator (LVN)—a free, open-source web app curating whole-child research and evidence-based strategies—to customize instruction for diverse learners. Found that even brief use of the tool encouraged teachers to apply research-based strategies addressing learner variability across PreK–12 classrooms.

entries.title: Assisting Students Struggling with Mathematics: Response to Intervention (RtI) for Elementary and Middle Schools
entries.desc: Eight recommendations for elementary and middle school math intervention using the Response to Intervention framework: screen all students and provide targeted support (Moderate), emphasize whole numbers K–5 and rational numbers grades 4–8 (Minimal), use explicit and systematic instruction (Strong), teach problem solving via common structural patterns (Strong), incorporate visual representations (Moderate), build fact fluency with approximately 10 minutes per session (Moderate), monitor student progress throughout intervention (Minimal), and include motivational strategies in secondary and tertiary intervention tiers (Minimal).

entries.title: Assisting Students Struggling with Reading: Response to Intervention (RtI) and Multi-Tier Intervention in the Primary Grades
entries.desc: Five recommendations for primary-grade reading intervention using a multi-tier approach: screen all students for reading problems at the beginning and middle of the year (Moderate), provide differentiated reading instruction based on current assessments (Minimal), deliver intensive systematic instruction on up to three foundational reading skills in small groups for below-benchmark students (Strong), monitor tier 2 student progress monthly (Minimal), and deliver daily intensive instruction to tier 3 students showing minimal progress (Minimal).

entries.title: Reducing Behavior Problems in the Elementary School Classroom
entries.desc: Five recommendations for elementary classroom behavior management: identify specific conditions triggering problem behavior (Moderate), modify the classroom environment to remove triggers (Strong), actively teach socially and behaviorally appropriate replacement skills (Strong), leverage relationships with colleagues, families, and behavioral experts (Moderate), and implement schoolwide prevention strategies requiring shared responsibility (Moderate).

entries.title: Encouraging Girls in Math and Science
entries.desc: Five recommendations for broadening girls' participation in STEM: teach that academic abilities are expandable and improvable (Moderate), provide prescriptive informational feedback about student performance (Moderate), introduce female role models in math and science fields (Minimal), create learning environments that build curiosity and avoid gender stereotypes (Moderate), and integrate spatial skills training into instruction (Minimal).

entries.title: Effective Literacy and English Language Instruction for English Learners in the Elementary Grades
entries.desc: Five recommendations for literacy instruction with elementary English learners: screen for reading problems and monitor progress in English (Strong), provide intensive small-group reading interventions for at-risk students (Strong), deliver extensive and varied vocabulary instruction throughout the school day (Strong), make formal academic English development a key instructional goal beginning in primary grades (Minimal), and schedule regular peer-assisted learning for structured academic collaboration approximately 90 minutes weekly (Strong).

entries.title: Turning Around Chronically Low-Performing Schools
entries.desc: Four recommendations for school turnaround leaders: signal the need for dramatic change with leadership communicating urgency to staff and stakeholders; maintain a consistent focus on improving instruction by leveraging data to set goals and reassess learning continuously; make visible improvements early in the turnaround process to motivate staff and reduce resistance; and build a committed staff aligned with improvement objectives.

entries.title: Improving Adolescent Literacy: Effective Classroom and Intervention Practices
entries.desc: Five recommendations for grades 5–12 literacy: provide explicit vocabulary instruction (Strong), deliver direct and explicit comprehension strategy instruction (Strong), facilitate extended discussion of text meaning and interpretation (Moderate), increase student motivation and engagement with reading (Moderate), and provide intensive individualized reading interventions by trained specialists for struggling readers (Strong).

entries.title: Structuring Out-of-School Time to Improve Academic Achievement
entries.desc: Five recommendations for administrators and educators designing out-of-school programs: align the OST program academically with the school day (Minimal), maximize student participation and attendance (Minimal), adapt instruction to individual and small group needs (Moderate), provide engaging learning experiences (Minimal), and assess program performance using results to continuously improve quality (Minimal).

entries.title: Helping Students Navigate the Path to College: What High Schools Can Do
entries.desc: Five recommendations for high schools: provide college-preparatory coursework and ensure ninth graders understand college-readiness requirements; use ongoing assessments to monitor readiness and address skill gaps; foster college aspirations through supportive adult and peer networks; facilitate completion of essential college application steps (Moderate); and enhance financial literacy and support the financial aid application process (Moderate).

entries.title: Improving Reading Comprehension in Kindergarten Through 3rd Grade
entries.desc: Five recommendations for K–3 reading comprehension: train students to apply reading comprehension strategies (Strong), instruct students to leverage text structure for comprehension and retention (Moderate), facilitate substantive dialogue about textual meaning (Minimal), choose materials strategically to foster comprehension growth (Minimal), and establish an engaging and motivating context for reading instruction (Moderate).

entries.title: Developing Effective Fractions Instruction for Kindergarten Through 8th Grade
entries.desc: Five recommendations for fractions instruction grades K–8: build on students' informal understanding of sharing and proportionality; establish that fractions are numbers that expand the number system beyond whole numbers; support understanding of the reasoning behind fraction computation procedures; develop conceptual understanding of ratio, rate, and proportion before introducing cross-multiplication; and improve teachers' own fraction knowledge through professional development. Emphasizes number lines as a central teaching tool across grade levels.

entries.title: Teaching Elementary School Students to Be Effective Writers
entries.desc: Four recommendations for grades 1–6 writing instruction: provide daily time for students to write (Minimal), teach students to use the writing process for a variety of purposes (Strong), teach fluency with handwriting, spelling, sentence construction, typing, and word processing (Moderate), and create an engaged community of writers (Minimal).

entries.title: Foundational Skills to Support Reading for Understanding in Kindergarten Through 3rd Grade
entries.desc: Four recommendations for K–3 foundational reading: teach academic language skills including inferential language and vocabulary (Minimal), develop phonological awareness and sound-letter links (Strong), teach decoding, word analysis, and written word recognition (Strong), and ensure each student reads connected text daily to build accuracy, fluency, and comprehension (Moderate).

entries.title: Teaching Secondary Students to Write Effectively
entries.desc: Three recommendations for grades 6–12 writing instruction across all subject areas: explicitly teach appropriate writing strategies using a Model-Practice-Reflect instructional cycle (Strong), integrate writing and reading to emphasize key writing features (Moderate), and use assessments of student writing to inform instruction and feedback (Minimal).

entries.title: Strategies for Postsecondary Students in Developmental Education
entries.desc: Six recommendations for college administrators, advisors, and faculty serving academically underprepared postsecondary students: use multiple measures to assess readiness and place students (Minimal), enhance advising through required or incentivized regular participation (Moderate), implement monetary performance incentives (Moderate), redesign developmental education through compression or mainstreaming (Minimal), teach self-regulated learning strategies (Minimal), and create comprehensive integrated support programs (Moderate).

entries.title: Preventing Dropout in Secondary Schools
entries.desc: Updated version of the 2008 dropout prevention guide, incorporating nine additional years of research. Four recommendations for grades 6–12: monitor all students and proactively intervene on early signs of attendance, behavior, or academic problems (Minimal); deliver tailored support to students who have fallen behind and face substantial barriers (Moderate); involve students through educational offerings bridging learning to college and career while building resilience (Strong); and for schools with many at-risk students, establish small personalized learning communities for better tracking and assistance (Moderate).

entries.title: Designing and Delivering Career Pathways at Community Colleges
entries.desc: Five recommendations for community college administrators, faculty, and staff: structure pathways enabling students to advance education, secure employment, and progress professionally (Moderate); provide contextualized basic skills training accelerating pathway entry and completion (Moderate); offer flexible schedules and instructional models boosting credential completion (Minimal); coordinate comprehensive student services to enhance credit accumulation (Moderate); and develop strategic employer and community partnerships preparing students for labor market success (Moderate).

entries.title: Effective Advising for Postsecondary Students
entries.desc: Four recommendations for postsecondary advising: design comprehensive advising combining academic and non-academic supports (Moderate); build sustained, personalized advisor-student relationships throughout the college experience (Strong); employ mentoring and coaching to strengthen advising and support student achievement (Strong); and integrate positive incentives into advising structures to boost sustained student participation (Strong).

entries.title: Providing Reading Interventions for Students in Grades 4–9
entries.desc: Four recommendations for grades 4–9 reading intervention: build students' decoding skills for complex multisyllabic words (Strong), provide purposeful fluency-building activities (Strong), implement interconnected comprehension practices covering vocabulary, question-and-answer activities, text-gist routines, and self-monitoring (Strong), and provide opportunities for students to practice with challenging stretch text (Moderate).

entries.title: Knowledge is Power Program (KIPP) Charter Schools
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on KIPP, a nationwide network of free open-enrollment college-preparatory schools in under-resourced communities providing approximately 60% more instructional time than traditional public schools. Reviews 4 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on mathematics and English language arts achievement, potentially positive effects on science and social studies achievement, and no discernible effects on student progression for grades 5–12.

entries.title: Cognitive Tutor® Algebra I
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Cognitive Tutor® Algebra I, published by Carnegie Learning — a math curriculum combining textbooks with adaptive intelligent tutoring software. Reviews 6 studies meeting standards out of 18 eligible. Found positive effects on algebra achievement (grades 8–PS, Tier 1) and no discernible effects on general mathematics achievement in later grades (9–10).

entries.title: Intelligent Tutoring for Structure Strategy (ITSS)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on ITSS, a web-based supplemental program teaching students to identify and use text structure patterns (comparison, cause-effect, sequence, description, problem-solution) to improve literacy comprehension and recall in grades K–8. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards. Found positive effects on comprehension (grades 4–7, Tier 1).

entries.title: Open Learning Initiative (OLI)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Carnegie Mellon University's Open Learning Initiative, offering innovative online courses designed to create high-quality learning experiences and advance educational research. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found mixed effects on academic achievement and potentially positive effects on credit accumulation and persistence for postsecondary students.

entries.title: DreamBox Learning
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on DreamBox Learning, a supplemental online adaptive mathematics program for grades K–5 that individualizes instruction by creating unique paths through the curriculum matching each student's level of comprehension and learning style. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on general mathematics achievement (K–1, Tier 3), based on a predominantly Hispanic, English learner, urban sample.

entries.title: READ 180®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on READ 180®, a reading program combining online and direct instruction, student assessment, and teacher professional development for struggling readers 2+ years below grade level in grades 4–10. Reviews 9 studies meeting standards out of 39 eligible. Found positive effects on comprehension and general literacy achievement, potentially positive effects on reading fluency, and no discernible effects on alphabetics.

entries.title: Achieve3000®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Achieve3000®, a supplemental online literacy program providing differentiated nonfiction reading content for grades preK–12, targeting phonemic awareness, phonics, fluency, comprehension, vocabulary, and writing. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 5 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on comprehension and general literacy achievement for adolescent readers (Tier 2).

entries.title: Success for All®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Success for All® (SFA®), a whole-school reform model integrating curriculum, school culture, family, and community supports, emphasizing phonics for beginning readers and comprehension for all students in grades K–4. Reviews 9 studies meeting standards out of 49 eligible. Found positive effects on alphabetics, potentially positive effects on reading fluency, and mixed effects on comprehension and general reading achievement.

entries.title: Self-Regulated Strategy Development (SRSD)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on SRSD, an intervention for grades 2–12 that improves academic skills through a six-step process teaching specific academic strategies and self-regulation, progressing from teacher-directed instruction to independent student application. Reviews 10 studies meeting standards out of 16 eligible. Based on single-case design studies, found potentially positive effects on writing achievement for students with specific learning disabilities (Tier 3).

entries.title: Growth Mindset (Postsecondary)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report reviewing growth mindset programs at the postsecondary level — interventions encouraging students to view intelligence as adaptable and academic challenges as surmountable. Reviews 6 studies meeting standards out of 10 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on academic achievement (Tier 2) and no discernible effects on college enrollment or progressing in college.

entries.title: Accelerated Study in Associate Programs (ASAP)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on ASAP, a three-year program removing barriers to associate degree completion by providing financial, academic, and personal supports while requiring full-time enrollment. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 4 eligible. Found positive effects on access/enrollment, degree attainment, and credit accumulation/persistence (Tier 1 across all three outcome domains).

entries.title: Dual Enrollment Programs
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on dual enrollment programs allowing high school students to take college courses and earn college credits, aimed at enhancing college access and degree completion for underrepresented students in grades 9–12. Reviews 5 studies meeting standards out of 35 eligible. Found positive effects (Tier 1) on degree attainment, college access/enrollment, credit accumulation, high school completion, and high school academic achievement.

entries.title: Head Start
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Head Start, the national federally-funded program providing education, health, nutrition, family engagement, and social services to promote school readiness for children ages 3–4 from predominantly low-income families. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 40 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on reading achievement and no discernible effects on mathematics or social-emotional development.

entries.title: Leveled Literacy Intervention
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Leveled Literacy Intervention, a short-term supplementary small-group literacy program for grades K–2 struggling readers delivering explicit instruction in phonological awareness, phonics, fluency, vocabulary, comprehension, oral language, and writing. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 10 eligible. Found positive effects on general reading achievement (Tier 1), potentially positive effects on reading fluency, and no discernible effects on alphabetics.

entries.title: Peer-Assisted Learning Strategies (PALS) — Beginning Reading
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on PALS, a peer-tutoring program for grades K–6 in which students take turns acting as tutor and tutee, coaching and correcting one another through literacy problems. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 13 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on alphabetics (K–1, Tier 2), no discernible effects on reading fluency, and mixed effects on comprehension.

entries.title: Good Behavior Game
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Good Behavior Game, a classroom management strategy for grades K–11 that organizes students into teams and rewards appropriate conduct to improve social skills and minimize disruptive behaviors. Reviews 16 studies meeting standards out of 31 eligible. Found positive effects on student behavior and teacher practice, potentially positive effects on writing conventions and writing productivity, and uncertain effects on academic achievement and school climate.

entries.title: Everyday Mathematics®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Everyday Mathematics®, a core PreK–6 curriculum emphasizing real-life problem solving, student communication of mathematical thinking, technology use, collaborative learning, and parent involvement. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 34 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on general mathematics achievement for grades 3–5.

entries.title: Career Academies
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Career Academies, school-within-school high school programs where students take integrated career and academic courses while gaining real work experience through employer partnerships. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 9 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on school completion and staying in school, and no discernible effects on academic progress.

entries.title: Check & Connect
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Check & Connect, a dropout prevention strategy for grades 9–12 combining close monitoring of school performance with mentoring, case management, and individualized student supports. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found positive effects on staying in school, potentially positive effects on progressing in school, and no discernible effects on completing school for students with learning, behavioral, or emotional disabilities.

entries.title: Teach for America (TFA)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Teach for America, a highly selective alternative route to teacher certification placing non-traditionally trained teachers in high-need public schools with 5–7 weeks of summer training plus ongoing professional development and coaching. Reviews 7 studies meeting standards out of 24 eligible. Found positive effects on mathematics achievement, potentially positive effects on science achievement, and no discernible effects on English language arts or social studies for grades preK–12.

entries.title: MyTeachingPartner-Secondary (MTP-S)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on MTP-S, a web-based professional development program for grades 6–12 providing teachers access to a video library of high-quality teaching examples and individualized coaching approximately twice monthly, using the CLASS-S framework to define and observe effective teaching practice. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on general academic achievement for middle and high school students (Tier 3).

entries.title: Science Teachers Learning from Lesson Analysis (STeLLA)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on STeLLA, a professional development program by BSCS Science Learning for grades 4–5 teachers that improves student science achievement by enhancing teachers' science content knowledge and ability to explain concepts, identify learning objectives, and engage students in scientific thinking. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on science achievement compared with another yearlong teacher PD program (Tier 3).

entries.title: Dual Language Programs
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on dual language programs providing content and literacy instruction through two languages (English and a partner language) in grades K–8, targeting academic achievement, bilingualism, biliteracy, and sociocultural competence. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on English literacy achievement (Tier 2) and uncertain effects on mathematics and science achievement.

entries.title: Fraction Face-Off!
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Fraction Face-Off!, a supplemental math program for grade 4 students needing additional support with fraction problems, delivered individually or in small groups by teachers. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on general mathematics achievement, geometry and measurement, and number and operations (Tier 3), with a diverse sample (52% Black, 88% free/reduced-price lunch, 15% English learners).

entries.title: Year Up
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Year Up, a postsecondary program providing six months of occupational and technical training in information technology and financial services followed by six-month internships, targeting youth in under-resourced communities. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards. Found positive effects on short-term earnings but no discernible effects on medium-term earnings, employment outcomes, or credential completion.

entries.title: Project QUEST (Quality Employment through Skills Training)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Project QUEST, a postsecondary workforce program providing comprehensive support services to help participants complete community college occupational training, pass certification exams, and obtain well-paying jobs in targeted sectors. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards. Found positive effects on credential/certificate completion, potentially positive effects on credit accumulation, and no discernible effects on employment or earnings outcomes.

entries.title: Integrated Basic Education and Skills Training (I-BEST)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on I-BEST, Washington State's program integrating basic skills and occupational training so students complete workforce training faster than traditional programs with wraparound supports. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on credential/certificate/license completion (Tier 1), potentially positive effects on short-term earnings and employment, and no discernible effects on credit accumulation.

entries.title: Social Skills Training (Early Childhood, Children with Disabilities)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on social skills training for preschool children with disabilities — a collection of behavioral practices teaching age-appropriate communication, problem solving, decision making, self-management, and peer relations in early childhood education settings. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on social-emotional development and no discernible effects on cognition.

entries.title: Repeated Reading
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Repeated Reading, an academic practice for grades 5–12 in which students read passages aloud multiple times with teacher support in a one-on-one setting to increase oral reading fluency. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 15 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on reading comprehension for students with learning disabilities (Tier 3) and no discernible effects on alphabetics, reading fluency, or general reading achievement.

entries.title: Single Stop
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Single Stop, a community college program connecting students and their families to public benefits through screening, application support, and wraparound services delivered at on-campus locations. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on academic achievement and progressing in college (Tier 2) for a predominantly Hispanic community college student population.

entries.title: Reading Recovery®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Reading Recovery®, a one-on-one tutoring intervention for grade 1 students with low literacy achievement delivering 30-minute daily sessions over 12–20 weeks, tailoring instruction in phonemic awareness, phonics, vocabulary, fluency, comprehension, writing, and oral language. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 20 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on literacy achievement, writing productivity, and receptive communication skills, with uncertain long-term effects on mathematics and academic achievement.

entries.title: Reading Apprenticeship®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Reading Apprenticeship®, a professional development program for grades 7–12 teachers that improves student literacy and also targets social-emotional outcomes including belonging, growth mindset, and self-efficacy. Reviews 5 studies meeting standards out of 9 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on science achievement and grade point average for grade 9 students (Tier 2) and uncertain effects on other literacy, mathematics, social studies, and behavioral outcomes.

entries.title: eMINTS Comprehensive Program
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on the eMINTS Comprehensive Program, a professional development program for grades 4–8 teachers offering structured PD, coaching, and support for integrating technology into the classroom. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 8 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on general mathematics achievement (Tier 2) and no discernible effects on literacy achievement.

entries.title: Building Blocksâ"¢
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Building Blocksâ"¢, a PreK mathematics curriculum developing children's knowledge of mathematics through learning trajectories, including whole-group and small-group instruction, center activities, computer activities, and family engagement. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on mathematics achievement (Tier 2) for a predominantly low-income, Black student population.

entries.title: Dana Center Mathematics Pathways
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Dana Center Mathematics Pathways, a postsecondary program offering multiple math pathways aligned to programs of study, accelerated enrollment in credit-bearing college math, integrated student supports, and evidence-based curricula and pedagogy. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found positive effects (Tier 1) on both college progression and progression through developmental education coursework.

entries.title: Bottom Line
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Bottom Line, an intensive advising program for high school students from low-income households covering college applications, financial aid, and institution selection, plus ongoing campus support for up to six years. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on college enrollment and college progression for low-income and first-generation college-going students (Tier 3).

entries.title: Summer Bridge Programs
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on summer bridge programs, 2–4 week postsecondary transition programs providing college orientation, academic advising, college success skills training, and accelerated coursework to ease the high school-to-college transition. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on grades and attainment (Tier 3) for a predominantly Black male student sample.

entries.title: I CAN Learn® Primary Mathematics
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on I CAN Learn® Primary Mathematics, a computer-based math curriculum for middle school through college delivering mastery-based interactive lessons that students complete individually at their own pace. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 7 eligible. Found no discernible effects on general mathematics achievement for eighth-grade students.

entries.title: Fast Track: Elementary School
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Fast Track: Elementary School, a comprehensive multi-component intervention including the PATHS curriculum, parent groups, social skills training, home visiting, peer-pairing, and academic tutoring to reduce conduct problems and improve academic, behavioral, and social outcomes. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on emotional/internal behavior, external behavior, reading achievement, and social outcomes for children with or at risk for emotional disturbance (Tier 3).

entries.title: World of Words (WOW)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on World of Words (WOW), a supplementary PreK curriculum developing vocabulary, concept knowledge, and content knowledge in science through intentional conversations and shared book readings centered on science topics. Reviews 4 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on language development (Tier 1) across a diverse, predominantly low-income sample.

entries.title: Fast ForWord®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Fast ForWord®, a computer-based reading program running 30–100 minutes daily for 4–16 weeks that develops the cognitive skills necessary for successful reading and learning in grades K–10. Reviews 8 studies meeting standards out of 29 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on reading fluency and comprehension, and no discernible effects on alphabetics or general literacy achievement for adolescent learners.

entries.title: Headsprout® Early Reading
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Headsprout® Early Reading, an online supplemental early literacy curriculum of eighty 20-minute animated episodes teaching phonemic awareness, phonics, fluency, vocabulary, and comprehension with adaptive instruction for PreK learners. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 2 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on oral language and print knowledge (Tier 3) in a predominantly Black, low-income Florida sample.

entries.title: Lexia Reading
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Lexia Reading, a computerized reading program providing phonics instruction and independent practice in basic reading skills for grades K–1 across five areas identified by the National Reading Panel. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 4 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on alphabetics and comprehension, and no discernible effects on reading fluency or general reading achievement.

entries.title: InsideTrack© Coaching
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on InsideTrack© Coaching, a postsecondary program providing proactive, personalized coaching to help students identify and overcome academic and non-academic barriers to college persistence and graduation. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 8 eligible. Found no discernible effects on grades/attainment and potentially positive effects on credit accumulation and persistence.

entries.title: First Year Experience Courses
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on first year experience courses, structured introductory courses for first-year students at 2- and 4-year colleges designed to enhance academic performance, social development, persistence, and degree completion. Reviews 4 studies meeting standards out of 97 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on academic achievement, degree attainment, and credit accumulation/persistence (Tiers 2–3).

entries.title: Pre-K Mathematics
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Pre-K Mathematics, a supplemental curriculum for preschool children developing informal mathematical knowledge and skills through small-group teacher-guided activities using concrete manipulatives and take-home activities for caregiver involvement. Reviews 5 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on mathematics achievement (Tier 1) and uncertain effects on language, literacy, self-regulation, and social-emotional learning.

entries.title: Class-Wide Function-Related Intervention Teams (CW-FIT)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on CW-FIT, a classroom management strategy for PreK–10 designed to create a positive learning environment by decreasing disruptive behavior and improving students' social skills through team-based incentive structures. Reviews 8 studies meeting standards out of 19 eligible. Found positive effects on student behavior (Tier 1) and positive effects on teacher practice (Tier 3).

entries.title: Job Corps
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Job Corps, a federally funded residential education and job training program for economically disadvantaged youth in grades 11–12 offering remedial education, GED preparation, vocational training, job placement, and other supports for up to 2 years. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on school completion and no discernible effects on academic progression.

entries.title: Success Boston Coaching
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Success Boston Coaching, a coaching intervention for traditionally underrepresented college students pairing them with dedicated coaches from spring of senior year through the first two years of college to support the high school-to-college transition. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on academic achievement and progressing in college (Tier 2) for a predominantly Black and Hispanic, low-income student population.

entries.title: ACT/SAT Test Preparation and Coaching Programs
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on ACT/SAT test preparation programs for grades 10–12 providing test familiarization, general and specific test-taking strategies, and practice drills aimed at increasing scores on college entrance exams. Reviews 6 studies meeting standards out of 26 eligible. Found positive effects on general academic achievement for high school students (Tier 1).

entries.title: University of Chicago School Mathematics Project (UCSMP) Algebra
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on UCSMP Algebra, a one-year course for grades 7–10 emphasizing algebra applications, using statistics and geometry to develop linear equations and inequalities with intentional connections across mathematical domains. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on algebra and general mathematics achievement (Tier 3).

entries.title: Earobics®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Earobics®, interactive software providing students in PreK–3 with individual systematic instruction in early literacy skills through animated characters. Reviews 4 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on alphabetics and potentially positive effects on reading fluency.

entries.title: Dialogic Reading
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Dialogic Reading for early childhood education children with disabilities, an interactive shared picture book reading practice where adults and children switch roles — with the child becoming the storyteller — to enhance language and literacy skills. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on communication/language outcomes (Tier 3) for children with disabilities.

entries.title: First Step to Success
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on First Step to Success, an early intervention program for grades K–3 at-risk children employing a trained behavior coach across three modules — screening, classroom intervention, and parent training — to prevent aggressive or antisocial behavioral patterns. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on external behavior, potentially positive effects on emotional/internal behavior, social outcomes, and academic performance, and no discernible effects on reading achievement.

entries.title: Reading Mastery
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Reading Mastery, a systematic reading instruction program for grades K–6 available as an intervention for struggling readers, a supplement to core programs, or a standalone curriculum, with students grouped by reading level and continuous progress monitoring. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 7 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on reading fluency and no discernible effects on comprehension for adolescent learners (Tier 3).

entries.title: Summer Counseling (Transition to College)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on summer counseling programs delivered between high school graduation and college enrollment via text messaging, email, phone, in-person meetings, and social media to help students complete enrollment tasks and overcome barriers to college attendance. Reviews 5 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on credit accumulation and persistence, and mixed effects on college access and enrollment.

entries.title: Core-Plus Mathematics
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Core-Plus Mathematics, a 4-year high school curriculum replacing traditional course sequences with interwoven strands of algebra and functions, statistics and probability, geometry and trigonometry, and discrete mathematics emphasizing modeling, technology, and collaborative reasoning. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 10 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on mathematics achievement for high school students (Tier 3).

entries.title: Cooperative Integrated Reading and Composition (CIRC)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on CIRC, a comprehensive reading and writing program for grades 2–8 incorporating story-related activities, direct reading comprehension instruction, and integrated language arts activities through cooperative pair and team learning strategies. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 5 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on comprehension and no discernible effects on general reading achievement for beginning readers.

entries.title: Sound Partners
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Sound Partners, a phonics-based tutoring program providing supplemental reading instruction to grades K–3 students with below-average reading skills. Reviews 7 studies meeting standards out of 11 eligible. Found positive effects on alphabetics and reading fluency (Tier 3), potentially positive effects on comprehension, and no discernible effects on general reading achievement.

entries.title: Phonological Awareness Training (Early Childhood)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on phonological awareness training for PreK children with disabilities, a general practice teaching children to identify, detect, delete, segment, or blend segments of spoken words and to detect and produce rhyme or alliteration. Reviews 4 studies meeting standards out of 13 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on communication/language competencies for children with learning disabilities in early education settings (Tier 3).

entries.title: Ladders to Literacy
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Ladders to Literacy, a supplemental kindergarten early literacy curriculum organizing activities into three sections: print awareness, phonological awareness, and oral language skills. Reviews 4 studies meeting standards out of 5 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on alphabetics and fluency, and mixed effects on comprehension across predominantly Midwestern and Southern school samples.

entries.title: Early Intervention in Reading (EIR)®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on EIR®, a program providing extra instruction to small groups of grades K–4 students at risk of reading failure, using picture books emphasizing phonemic awareness, phonics, contextual analysis, repeated reading, and writing. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 7 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on alphabetics and comprehension (Tier 3).

entries.title: Lindamood Phoneme Sequencing® (LiPS®)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on LiPS®, a program teaching phonemic awareness and decoding skills to grades 1–4 students through activities focused on mouth movements and sound production, designed for struggling readers. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 8 eligible. Found mixed effects on alphabetics and potentially positive effects on comprehension for beginning readers.

entries.title: Voyager Universal Literacy System®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Voyager Universal Literacy System®, a core reading program for kindergarten students using individual instruction, comprehension activities, problem solving, writing, and computer-based practice to help students reach grade-level reading by end of grade 3. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 5 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on alphabetics and potentially negative effects on comprehension.

entries.title: UCSMP Multiple Courses
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on University of Chicago School Mathematics Project (UCSMP) Multiple Courses, a core mathematics curriculum for grades 7–10 emphasizing problem solving, real-world applications, technology use, and student-centered active learning with flexible lesson organization. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 4 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on general mathematics achievement (Tier 3).

entries.title: Linked Learning Communities
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Linked Learning Communities, postsecondary programs providing social and curricular linkages giving undergraduate students intentional integration of learning themes through community-based active learning. Reviews 4 studies meeting standards out of 16 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on academic achievement (Tier 2) and no discernible effects on enrollment, degree attainment, credit accumulation, or developmental education progression.

entries.title: McREL Balanced Leadership®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on McREL Balanced Leadership®, a professional development program for K–12 school principals and aspiring school leaders involving facilitated sessions over one to two years with optional coaching support. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on school leader retention (Tier 3).

entries.title: Pathway to Academic Success
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Pathway to Academic Success, a program training grades 6–12 teachers to improve English learners' reading and writing by incorporating cognitive strategies — goal setting, prior knowledge activation, questioning, predictions, and writing evaluation — into literacy instruction. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 5 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on writing quality (Tier 2), writing conventions, and general literacy achievement for a predominantly Hispanic English learner population.

entries.title: Read Naturally®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Read Naturally®, a supplemental reading program for grades 2–6 using a combination of books, audiotapes, and computer software with repeated reading, teacher modeling, and progress monitoring strategies to improve reading fluency. Reviews 5 studies meeting standards out of 11 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on general reading achievement, mixed effects on reading fluency, and no discernible effects on alphabetics or comprehension.

entries.title: Corrective Reading
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Corrective Reading, a program for grades 3–5 students reading below grade level using sequenced, scripted lessons in small groups or whole-class formats to promote reading accuracy, fluency, and comprehension. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on reading fluency and no discernible effects on alphabetics or comprehension.

entries.title: Bright Beginnings
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Bright Beginnings, an early childhood curriculum for at-risk preschoolers based partly on High/Scope and Creative Curriculum, emphasizing literacy skills through nine thematic units targeting cognitive, social, emotional, and physical development. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 2 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on print knowledge and no discernible effects on oral language, phonological processing, or mathematics (Tier 3).

entries.title: National Guard Youth ChalleNGe Program
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on National Guard Youth ChalleNGe, a residential education and training program for youth ages 16–18 who have dropped out of or been expelled from high school, with a 22-week residential component including GED preparation and youth development followed by one year of structured mentoring. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 4 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on school completion (Tier 3).

entries.title: Failure Free Reading
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Failure Free Reading, a language development program for students in kindergarten through grade 12 scoring in the bottom 15% on standardized tests who have not responded to conventional reading instruction, targeting vocabulary, fluency, word recognition, and comprehension. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on comprehension and no discernible effects on alphabetics or reading fluency (Tier 3).

entries.title: Curiosity Corner
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Curiosity Corner, a comprehensive early childhood curriculum for 3- and 4-year-olds at risk of school failure due to poverty, emphasizing language and literacy skills through sequential daily activities. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on oral language and no discernible effects on print knowledge, phonological processing, cognition, or mathematics (Tier 3).

entries.title: Reading Plus®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Reading Plus®, a web-based reading intervention for grades 3+ providing individualized scaffolded silent reading practice to develop silent reading fluency, comprehension, and vocabulary with difficulty automatically adjusted to student skill level. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 5 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on comprehension for adolescent learners (Tier 3) in a predominantly Hispanic and Black urban sample.

entries.title: Ready, Set, Leap!®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Ready, Set, Leap!®, a comprehensive preschool curriculum emphasizing phonemic awareness and letter knowledge through multi-sensory technology incorporating touch, sight, and sound. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on phonological processing, no discernible effects on most literacy domains, and potentially negative effects on mathematics (Tier 3).

entries.title: SpellReadâ"¢
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on SpellRead™, a literacy program for struggling readers in grade 2 or above — including special education students and English learners — integrating auditory and visual reading components through systematic, explicit instruction. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 2 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on alphabetics, reading fluency, and comprehension for adolescent readers (Tier 3).

entries.title: Waterford Early Reading Programâ"¢
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Waterford Early Reading Program™, a software-based curriculum for grades K–2 promoting reading, writing, and typing through letter mastery, spelling, and comprehension strategies. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 14 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on alphabetics and no discernible effects on comprehension (Tier 3).

entries.title: Project CRISS®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Project CRISS® (CReating Independence through Student-owned Strategies), a professional development program for grades 3–12 teachers focusing on three principles: student self-monitoring of comprehension, integration of new with prior knowledge, and active engagement through discussion and writing. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 4 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on comprehension for adolescent learners (Tier 3).

entries.title: Functional Behavioral Assessment-based Interventions
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Functional Behavioral Assessment-based Interventions for students with or at risk for emotional disturbance — an individualized problem-solving process identifying the function of problem behavior through environmental analysis, then implementing tailored interventions. Reviews 17 studies meeting standards out of 34 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on school engagement and problem behavior (Tier 3, single-case design studies).

entries.title: FLIGHT (Facilitating Long-term Improvements in Graduation and Higher Education for Tomorrow)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on FLIGHT, a grades 7–11 college preparation program for disadvantaged students based on partnership between a private non-profit (Taking Stock in Children) delivering mentoring and wraparound services. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on college access and enrollment and no discernible effects on high school attendance or academic achievement.

entries.title: Green Dot Public Schools
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Green Dot Public Schools, a nonprofit charter school network for grades 9–12 emphasizing high quality teaching, strong school leadership, college-preparatory curriculum, and community partnerships. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on mathematics achievement, student progression, school attendance, and English language arts achievement (Tier 3) for a predominantly Hispanic, low-income, English learner population.

entries.title: Early Risers
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Early Risers, a multi-year prevention program for grades K–2 children demonstrating early aggressive and disruptive behavior, combining child-focused skill development with parent/family support through group sessions and home visits. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on academic achievement, external behavior, and social outcomes, and no discernible effects on emotional/internal behavior.

entries.title: GEMS Space Science Sequence
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Great Explorations in Math and Science (GEMS) Space Science Sequence, a grades 3–5 physical science curriculum using models, hands-on investigations, peer discussions, reflection, and informational readings across four units on planetary concepts, Earth's movement, gravity, and moon phases. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on science achievement for grades 4–5 (Tier 3).

entries.title: Spelling Mastery
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Spelling Mastery, a Direct Instruction program for grades 1–6 explicitly teaching spelling through phonemic, morphemic, and whole-word strategies. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 4 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on writing achievement for students with specific learning disabilities (Tier 3).

entries.title: Building Blocks for Math (SRA Real Math)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Building Blocks for Math/SRA Real Math Building Blocks, a supplemental PreK mathematics curriculum embedding mathematical learning in daily activities — from designated math activities to circle and story time — to develop early mathematical knowledge through individual, small-group, and large-group activities. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found positive effects on mathematics achievement (Tier 3).

entries.title: Doors to Discoveryâ"¢
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Doors to Discoveryâ"¢, a preschool literacy curriculum using eight thematic units to develop oral language, phonological awareness, print concepts, alphabet knowledge, writing, and comprehension skills. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 4 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on oral language and print knowledge and no discernible effects on phonological processing or mathematics.

entries.title: JOBSTART
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on JOBSTART, an alternative education and training program for grades 11–12 young, disadvantaged high school dropouts providing at least 200 hours of basic education, 500 hours of occupational training, support services, and job placement assistance. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on school completion (Tier 3) in a predominantly Black and Hispanic sample.

entries.title: Red Light, Purple Light! (RLPL)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Red Light, Purple Light! (RLPL), a PreK classroom-based self-regulation intervention consisting of music and movement-based circle time games designed to systematically increase in cognitive complexity. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on self-regulation (Tier 2) and uncertain effects on mathematics, reading/literacy, and language.

entries.title: Odyssey® Math
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Odyssey® Math, a web-based mathematics program by Compass Learning® for grades K–8 providing curriculum and formative assessments to support differentiated, data-driven instruction through individualized learning paths. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 9 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on mathematics achievement (Tier 2).

entries.title: Open Court Reading©
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Open Court Reading©, a grades K–6 reading program teaching decoding, comprehension, inquiry, and writing through a three-part progression: phonemic awareness and phonics, reading literature with comprehension, and language arts including writing and grammar. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 30 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on comprehension (grades 1–5) and general reading achievement (grades 2–3, Tier 3).

entries.title: Technology Enhanced Elementary and Middle School Science (TEEMSS)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on TEEMSS, a grades 3–8 physical science curriculum using computers, sensors, and interactive models to support inquiry-based investigations of real-world phenomena through 15 units with handheld data collection and a web-based teacher-reporting tool for student portfolio review. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 2 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on science achievement for grades 3–4 (Tier 3).

entries.title: Accelerated Readerâ"¢
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Accelerated Reader™, a computerized supplementary reading program for grades K–12 providing guided reading practice and feedback aimed at improving reading skills. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 57 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on comprehension for grades 1–8 and no discernible effects on reading fluency.

entries.title: Wilson Reading System®
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Wilson Reading System®, a structured literacy program teaching the structure of English words to help struggling grade 3 students master reading and spelling through direct instruction in phonics and decoding. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on alphabetics and no discernible effects on reading fluency or comprehension.

entries.title: Read Well® (English Language Learners)
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Read Well® for English Language Learners, a grades K–1 reading curriculum combining phonemic awareness, phonics, vocabulary, comprehension, and fluency instruction through scaffolded whole-class and small-group lessons. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on English language development and no discernible effects on general reading achievement for ELL students in rural Colorado (Tier 3).

entries.title: New Chance
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on New Chance, a program for young welfare mothers who have dropped out of school, combining GED preparation, parenting and life skills curriculum, occupational training, and job placement assistance. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on school completion (Tier 3) in an all-female, predominantly Black sample across 10 states.

entries.title: Lovaas Model of Applied Behavior Analysis
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on the Lovaas Model of Applied Behavior Analysis, an intensive behavioral therapy for PreK children with disabilities delivering 35–40 hours weekly of one-on-one discrete trial instruction — brief periods where a teacher cues a behavior, prompts the appropriate response, and provides reinforcement — for approximately 3 years. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 8 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on cognitive development and no discernible effects on communication/language, social-emotional development, or functional abilities.

entries.title: Accelerated Middle Schools
entries.desc: WWC Intervention Report on Accelerated Middle Schools, self-contained academic programs for grades 6–8 students behind grade level enabling them to progress an additional year of curriculum within 1–2 years to keep them on track through high school. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards. Found positive effects on progressing in school and staying in school (predominantly Black, urban student population).

entries.title: Effective Teacher Professional Development
entries.desc: Learning Policy Institute research synthesis by Darling-Hammond, Hyler, and Gardner (2017) identifying seven features of effective teacher professional development across 35 rigorous studies. Finds effective PD is content-focused, incorporates active learning, supports collaboration, models effective practice, provides coaching and expert support, offers feedback and reflection, and is sustained over time. Concludes that PD meeting these criteria demonstrably improves teacher practice and student learning outcomes.

entries.title: Design Principles for Teacher Preparation: Enacting the Science of Learning and Development
entries.desc: Learning Policy Institute report by Darling-Hammond, Hyler, Wojcikiewicz, and Rushing (2025) presenting five evidence-based design principles for teacher preparation programs grounded in the science of learning and development. Principles address: curriculum grounded in developmental understanding of learners; equitable educator mindsets and skills; rich experiential learning with feedback cycles; pedagogical alignment between teacher educators and candidates; and supportive developmental relationships within programs and PreK–12 school partnerships. Draws on neuroscience, developmental psychology, and exemplary program practices.

entries.title: Community Schools Impact on Student Outcomes: Evidence From California
entries.desc: Learning Policy Institute study by Swain, Leung-Gagné, Maier, and Rubinstein (2025) evaluating California's Community Schools Partnership Program (CCSPP), a $4.1 billion state initiative. Analyzes 458 schools using matched difference-in-differences methodology across 2018–19 through 2023–24. Finds 30% greater reduction in chronic absenteeism, ~15% decline in suspensions, and academic gains of 0.06 SD in math and 0.05 SD in ELA (~43 and ~36 additional learning days). Benefits were disproportionately large for Black students, English learners, and socioeconomically disadvantaged students.

entries.title: The State of the Teacher Workforce
entries.desc: Learning Policy Institute interactive report (updated July 2024) providing state-by-state analysis of teacher workforce conditions across all 50 U.S. states. Assigns each state a teaching attractiveness rating (1–5) measuring compensation, working conditions, and retention factors, and a teacher equity rating (1–5) assessing equitable distribution of qualified educators across demographic groups. Draws from national datasets for 2017–18 through 2022–23 academic years.

entries.title: How People Learn: Brain, Mind, Experience, and School (Expanded Edition)
entries.source: National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine
entries.desc: National Research Council expanded edition report (2000) edited by Bransford, Brown, and Cocking synthesizing research on learning science across cognitive, developmental, and neuroscientific perspectives. Bridges theoretical findings with classroom practice by examining how expertise develops, how knowledge transfers, and how children acquire understanding. Provides evidence-based guidance for designing effective learning environments across history, mathematics, and science disciplines. Free online reading via National Academies Press.

entries.title: How People Learn II: Learners, Contexts, and Cultures
entries.source: National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine
entries.desc: National Academies consensus report (2018) chaired by Cora B. Marrett updating the influential 2000 How People Learn volume. Synthesizes findings from cognitive neuroscience, educational research, and learning technologies with emphasis on sociocultural and environmental factors shaping learning across the lifespan. Addresses learning from infancy through adulthood in both formal and informal settings. Free online reading via National Academies Press.

entries.title: CASEL SEL Framework
entries.source: Collaborative for Academic, Social, and Emotional Learning (CASEL)
entries.desc: CASEL framework (updated 2020) defining the five core social-emotional competencies — self-awareness, self-management, social awareness, relationship skills, and responsible decision-making — and their implementation across four settings: classrooms, schools, families and caregivers, and communities. The 2020 update strengthened the framework's equity orientation, emphasizing SEL's potential in promoting educational equity and excellence and its adaptability to local cultural contexts.

entries.title: Engaging First: Supporting Young Learners Through Family Engagement
entries.desc: EdTrust research brief by Ivy Morgan (2024) examining family-school partnerships for early elementary learners based on nationally representative surveys of 600 parents/caregivers and 300 teachers of first and second graders during 2022–23. Finds broadly positive communication overall but significant gaps: only ~two-thirds of parents had access to parent-teacher conferences; low-income and multilingual families faced greater barriers including unwelcoming school staff. Part of EdTrust's broader SEAD research program.

entries.title: Making Assessment Reports More Meaningful for Students & Families
entries.desc: EdTrust report by Munyan-Penney and Levitan (2024) providing guidance for schools on improving communication of federally required annual assessment results to students and families. Advocates for integrating score reports into existing family communications, identifies best practices for accessible report design, and offers policy recommendations for advocates and state leaders to improve assessment reporting at scale.

entries.title: How Student, Family, and Community Engagement Impacts Students' SEAD
entries.desc: EdTrust report by Duchesneau and Wriston (2024) examining how school, family, and community engagement supports students' social, emotional, and academic development (SEAD). Identifies obstacles to meaningful engagement and provides policy recommendations for federal, state, and local decision-makers. Part of EdTrust's broader research on whole-child development and the role of family-school partnerships in student wellbeing and academic outcomes.

entries.title: Funding Gaps 2018
entries.desc: EdTrust report by Morgan and Amerikaner (2018) documenting school funding disparities across U.S. states and districts. Finds districts serving predominantly students of color receive ~$1,800 (13%) less per student in state and local funding than majority-white districts; high-poverty districts receive ~$1,000 (7%) less than low-poverty districts. Gap widens to $2,000 per student (16%) when adjusted for additional costs of supporting low-income students. Documents state-by-state variation and highlights states achieving more equitable funding.

entries.title: Teacher Turnover in the United States: Who Moves, Who Leaves, and Why
entries.desc: This comprehensive analysis examines why approximately 1 in 7 public school teachers annually leave their schools or exit the profession, using national data from 2020–22. The report finds that turnover rates have increased roughly 27% since the early 1990s, with disparities concentrated in schools serving predominantly low-income students and students of color. Three primary factors significantly influence retention: teacher salaries, effective school leadership, and overall job satisfaction tied to classroom autonomy and collegial support.

entries.title: Supporting and Sustaining a Diverse Teacher Workforce
entries.desc: This report examines why teachers of color leave the profession at higher rates than their white counterparts and identifies systemic barriers to their retention. Using national survey data from 2017–2021, researchers found that teachers of color face less access to comprehensive preparation, competitive salaries, mentoring support, and positive working environments. The study emphasizes that while recruiting more educators of color matters, retention through improved conditions and support systems is equally critical to building a stable, diverse teaching force.

entries.title: Untangling the Evidence on Preschool Effectiveness: Insights for Policymakers
entries.desc: This report synthesizes rigorous evaluations of 21 public preschool programs to clarify conflicting findings about whether quality early childhood education produces meaningful gains for students. The research demonstrates that high-quality preschool leaves children better prepared for school, especially in academic skill development, with benefits extending into adulthood through higher graduation rates and earnings. The report identifies six essential quality elements—including small class sizes, well-trained teachers, developmentally appropriate curricula, and family engagement—and notes that effective programs generate up to $17 in social returns for every dollar invested.

entries.title: Community Schools as an Effective School Improvement Strategy: A Review of the Evidence
entries.desc: This comprehensive report synthesizes findings from 143 rigorous research studies examining how community schools impact student and school outcomes, identifying four core pillars of effective implementation: integrated student supports, expanded learning time, family and community engagement, and collaborative leadership. The study demonstrates that community schools help disadvantaged students overcome barriers to learning by integrating academic instruction with health services, youth development, and community partnerships. The evidence base meets federal requirements under the Every Student Succeeds Act, providing policymakers with research-backed guidance for implementing community schools as an equitable school improvement strategy.

entries.title: Coaching at Scale: A Strategy for Strengthening the Early Learning Workforce
entries.desc: This report examines five early childhood coaching systems across Alabama, Washington, and California to understand how comprehensive coaching can be implemented at scale to improve educator practice and program quality.
